questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $y^2=x$ be a given parabola and a variable chord cuts the parabola at $P$ and $Q$. Let $C$ be the vertex of parabola. If the locus of the point of intersection of tangents at $P$ and $Q$ is $x+1=0$, then the minimum area of triangle $P C Q$ be $M$. Find $4 M$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} a t_1 t_2 & =-1 \Rightarrow t_1 t_2=\frac{-1}{a}=-4 \\ \Delta & =\frac{1}{2}\left|\begin{array}{ccc}0 & 0 & 1 \\ a t_1^2 & 2 a t_1 & 1 \\ a t_2^2 & 2 a t_2 & 1\end{array}\right|=\frac{1}{2}\left(2 a^2 t_1^2 t_2-2 a^2 t_1 t_2^2\right) \\ \Delta & =\left|a^2 t_1 t_2\left(t_1-t_2\right)\right|=\frac{1}{4}\left|t_1-t_2\right| \\ \Delta & =\frac{1}{4}\left(t_1+\frac{4}{t_1}\right)=\frac{1}{4}\left[\left(\sqrt{t_1}-\frac{2}{\sqrt{t_1}}\right)^2+4\right] \\ \Delta_{\min .} & =1=M \\ 4 M & =4\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If the locus of the point from where two of three normals to the curve $y^2+2 y-4 x+5=0$ are perpendicular is $\left(y-\frac{k}{2}\right)^2-(x-\lambda)=0$, then find the value of $\lambda-2 k$.
questions.solution:
The curve is $(y+1)^2=4(x-1)$ equation of the normal to the given curve is $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{y}+1=\mathrm{m}(\mathrm{x}-1)-2 \mathrm{~m}-\mathrm{m}^3 \\ & \mathrm{y}=\mathrm{mx}-3 \mathrm{~m}-\mathrm{m}^3-1 \end{aligned}$ which passes through (h, k)
$\begin{aligned} & m_1 m_2 m_3=-(1+k) \\ & m_3=(1+k) \quad \because \quad m_1 m_2=-1 \\ & (1+k)^3+(1+k)(3-h)+(1+k)=0 \\ & (1+k)^2+3-h+1=0 \\ & (y+1)^2=x-4\end{aligned}$ $\therefore \quad$ locus of $(\mathrm{h}, \mathrm{k})$ are $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{C}_1:(\mathrm{y}+1)^2=\mathrm{x}-4 \\ & \frac{\mathrm{k}}{2}=-1 \quad \Rightarrow \quad \mathrm{k}=-2, \lambda=4 \\ & \therefore \quad \lambda-2 \mathrm{k}=8\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Let line $L_1$ be the reflection of a tangent to the parabola $(y-2)^2=4(x-1)$ drawn at $P$ in the line $x=1$.
If area of the triangle formed by the line $L_1$, the tangent and straight line $y=2$ is $64 \mathrm{sq}$. units, then find the abscissa of point $P$.
questions.solution: $$ \begin{aligned} y^2 & =4 x \\ & =P\left(a t^2, 2 a t\right) \end{aligned} $$ Tangent at $P$. $$ \begin{aligned} t Y & =X+t^2 \\ Q & \equiv\left(-t^2, 0\right), R \equiv\left(t^2, 0\right) \end{aligned} $$ Area of $\triangle P Q R, \frac{1}{2} \times 2 t^2 \times t= \pm 64$ $$ \Rightarrow \quad t= \pm 4 $$ Now, abscissa of point $P \Rightarrow x-1=16$ $$ \Rightarrow $$ $$ x=17 $$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If tangent drawn to the parabola $y^2=-a x(a>0)$ from a point $A(1,0)$ which also touches the hyperbola $\frac{x^2}{4}-\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1$ at $B$ such that $\angle A S B=90^{\circ}$, where $S$ is the focus of the hyperbola then find the value of $\left(a+b^2\right)$.
questions.solution: Tangent to the parabola $$ \begin{aligned} y & =m x-\frac{a}{4 m} \\ (1,0) \Rightarrow a & =4 m^2 \end{aligned} $$ $(1,0)$ lies on the directrix of the hyperbola $$ \frac{2}{e}=1 \Rightarrow e=2 $$ $$ \therefore \quad 1+\frac{b^2}{4}=4 \Rightarrow b^2=12 $$ Tangent to the hyperbola $$ \begin{aligned} & y=m x \pm \sqrt{4 m^2-b^2} \\ & \therefore \quad \frac{a^2}{16 m^2}=4 m^2-b^2 \\ & m^2=4 m^2-b^2 \Rightarrow 3 m^2=12 \Rightarrow m^2=4 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad a=16 \\ & \therefore \quad a+b^2=28 \\ & \end{aligned} $$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Suppose that a parabola $y=a x^2+b x+c$, where $a\gt0$ and $(a+b+c)$ is an integer has vertex $\left(\frac{1}{4}, \frac{-9}{8}\right)$. If the minimum possible value of 'a' can be written as $\frac{p}{q}$ where $p$ and $q$ are relatively prime positive integers, then find $(p+q)$
questions.solution:
The equation of the parabola can be taken as
$y=m\left(x-\frac{1}{4}\right)^2-\frac{9}{8}$ (given that minimum value is $\frac{-9}{8}$ when $x=\frac{1}{4}$ )
or $y=m\left(x^2+\frac{1}{16}-\frac{x}{2}\right)-\frac{9}{8}$
or $\mathrm{y}=\mathrm{mx}^2-\frac{\mathrm{m}}{2} \mathrm{x}+\frac{\mathrm{m}}{16}-\frac{9}{8}$ .....(1)
Comparing equation (1) with $y=a x^2+b x+c$,
Hence $a=m, b=\frac{-m}{2}$ and $c=\frac{m}{16}-\frac{9}{8}$
But $a+b+c$ is an integer
$\frac{m}{2}+\frac{m}{16}-\frac{9}{8}=\frac{9 m-18}{16}$ is an integer.
If $a+b+c=0 \Rightarrow m=2$
If $a+b+c=-1 \Rightarrow m=\frac{2}{9}$

If $a+b+c=-2 \Rightarrow m=\frac{-14}{9}$ which is negative

Hence minimum positive value of coefficient of $x^2$ which is $m=\frac{2}{9}$
$\Rightarrow p=2 \text { and } q=9$

Hence $(p+q)=11$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $P_0$ is the parabola $y^2=4 x$ with vertex $K(0,0), A$ and $B$ are points on $P_0$ where tangents drawn intersect at right angles. Let $C$ be the centroid of $\triangle A B K$. The locus of $C$ is another parabola $P_1$. Now the process is repeated with $P_1$ then $P_2, P_3 \ldots$ etc. Then the length of latus rectum of $P_{10}$ can be expressed as $\frac{a}{b}$ where $a, b$ are co-prime natural numbers. Find the value of $\left(a+\log _3 b\right)$.
questions.solution:
The answer is $\frac{4}{3^{10}}$, here is the solution.
Since tangents are at right angles, the line joining the point of contacts is the focal chord.
Here $a=1$
Let $A\left(t_1^2, 2 t_1\right)$ and $B\left(t_2^2, 2 t_2\right)$
Since, these are end points of focal chord $t_1 t_2=-1$
Let $t_1=t$
Point $A\left(t^2, 2 t\right)$ and $B\left(\frac{1}{t^2},-\frac{2}{t}\right)$
Let centroid $C(h, k)$ and given $K(0,0)$
$h=\frac{t^2+\frac{1}{t^2}+0}{3}$ ...(1)
and
$$
k=\frac{2 t-\frac{2}{t}+0}{3} \Rightarrow t-\frac{1}{t}=\frac{3 k}{2}
$$

From Eqn. (1), we have
$$
\begin{aligned}
3 h-2 & =\left(t-\frac{1}{t}\right)^2 \\
P_1 & =y^2=\frac{4}{3}\left(x-\frac{2}{3}\right)
\end{aligned}
$$

On repeating similar procedure
$$
\begin{gathered}
P_2=y^2=\frac{4}{3^2}\left(x-\frac{4}{9}\right) \text { thus } \\
P_{10}=y^2=\frac{4}{3^{10}}\left(x-\frac{2^{10}}{3^{10}}\right) \\
a+b=4+\log _3 3^{10}=4+10=14
\end{gathered}
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: If parabola with focus $\left(\frac{2}{5}, \frac{4}{5}\right)$ touches $x$ and $y$ axis at $A$ and $B$ respectively, then area of $\Delta O A B$ is, where $O$ is origin
questions.solution: If parabola touches at $x$-axis at $A(a, 0)$ and $y$-axis at $B(0, b)$, then focus is point of intersection of circles with diameter $O A$ and $O B$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: From origin tangents $O A$ and $O B$ are drawn to $(x-2)^2+(y-2)^2=1$. If line $P Q$ touches $(y+3)^2=4 a(x+4)$, if length of latus rectum of parabola is 1 , then $\frac{1}{7}$ is $\qquad$ (where $P$ and $Q$ are mid point of $O A$ and $O B$ )
questions.solution: The equation of $P Q$ is $4 x+4 y=7$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If all chords of the parabola $y^2=4 x+4$ which subtends a right angle at point $(1,2 \sqrt{2})$ passes through a fixed point $(\alpha, \beta)$, then the value of $|\alpha+\beta+2 \sqrt{2}+3|$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Shifting origin at $(1,2 \sqrt{2})$
$(Y+2 \sqrt{2})^2=4(X+1)+4$
Let chord is $Y=m X+c$
$Y^2+4 \sqrt{2} Y\left(\frac{Y-m X}{c}\right)-4 \times\left(\frac{Y-m X}{c}\right)=0$
Coefficient of $X^2+$ coefficient $Y^2=0$
$\begin{aligned}
& 1+\frac{4 \sqrt{2}}{c}+\frac{4 m}{c}=0 \\
& c+4 \sqrt{2}+4 m=0 \\
& Y=m X-4 \sqrt{2}-4 m \\
& (Y+4 \sqrt{2})-m(X-4)=0 \\
& X=4, Y=-4 \sqrt{2} \\
& X=5, y=2 \sqrt{2}-4 \sqrt{2} .
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The parabola $y=a x^2+b x+c$ does not meet the $x$-axis and passes through the points $A(-2,1)$ and $B(2,9)$. If range of the abscissa of vertex of parabola is $\left(x_1, x_2\right)$, then find the value of $\left|x_1+x_2\right|$
questions.solution:
Given, $y=a x^2+b x+c$ does not intersect the $x$-axis $\Rightarrow b^2-4 a c \lt 0$
$\begin{aligned}
& \text { Also, } 1=4 a-2 b+c \qquad .....(2) \\
& \text { and } 9=4 a+2 b+c \qquad .....(3) 
\end{aligned}$
Now, $(3)-(2) \Rightarrow 4 b=8 \Rightarrow b=2$
$\begin{array}{ll}
\therefore & 4 a+c=5 \qquad .....(4) \\
\Rightarrow & c=(5-4 a)
\end{array}$
Using, $b^2-4 a c \lt 0$
$\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow 4-4 a(5-4 a) \lt 0 \\
& \Rightarrow 4-20 a+16 a^2 \lt 0 \\
& \Rightarrow 1-5 a+4 a^2 \lt 0 \\
& \Rightarrow 4 a^2-5 a+1 \lt 0 \\
& \Rightarrow 4 a^2-4 a-a+1 \lt 0 \\
& \Rightarrow 4 a(a-1)-(a-1) \lt 0 \\
& \Rightarrow(a-1)(4 a-1) \lt 0
\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow \frac{1}{4} \lt \mathrm{a} \lt 1 \\
& \Rightarrow \frac{1}{2} \lt 2 \mathrm{a} \lt 2 \\
& \Rightarrow 1 \lt \frac{\mathrm{b}}{2 \mathrm{a}} \lt 4 \\
& \Rightarrow-4 \lt \frac{-\mathrm{b}}{2 \mathrm{a}} \lt -1 \\
& \Rightarrow \frac{-\mathrm{b}}{2 \mathrm{a}} \in(-4,-1)=\left(\mathrm{x}_1, \mathrm{x}_2\right)
\end{aligned}$
Hence, $\left|x_1+x_2\right|=5$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
